# How to Make Great Coffee

Making great coffee at home is easier than you think. With the right beans, equipment, and technique, you can brew a cup that rivals your favorite cafe. Here's everything you need to know to get started on your coffee journey.

## Choosing Your Beans

The foundation of any great cup of coffee starts with quality beans. Look for freshly roasted beans from a reputable roaster, ideally roasted within the last two weeks. Single-origin beans will give you distinct flavor profiles, while blends offer balanced, consistent taste.

![Fresh coffee beans](coffee-beans.jpg)

## The Brewing Process

Water temperature matters more than most people realize. Aim for water between 195-205 degrees Fahrenheit, which is just below boiling. Too hot and you'll over-extract, resulting in bitter coffee. Too cool and you'll under-extract, getting a weak, sour brew.

The grind size should match your brewing method. For pour-over, use a medium grind similar to sea salt. For French press, go coarser like breadcrumbs. For espresso, you need a fine grind like powdered sugar.

## Final Tips

Always use filtered water, as tap water minerals can dramatically affect taste. Store your beans in an airtight container away from light and heat. And most importantly, experiment with ratios until you find your perfect cup.
